topic.what: Comprehensive review of decentralised trust and security mechanisms for IoT networks at the edge
topic.how: Systematic analysis of 30 recent studies examining federated learning, blockchain, Zero Trust Architecture, deep neural networks, and multi-layer security frameworks
topic.why_matters: Traditional centralized security architectures are unsuitable for heterogeneous, resource-constrained IoT edge environments requiring autonomous, adaptive trust establishment without central oversight

characteristics.evidence: Selected from IEEE Xplore, SpringerLink, ACM Digital Library, ScienceDirect following inclusion criteria
characteristics.context: Review period 2021-2025, covering federated learning IDS, blockchain trust, ZTA, DNN-based detection, decentralized identity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Intrusion detection performance across reviewed systems
characteristics.evidence: SecFedDNN, CatBoost IDS, DeepTrust, NeuroTrust report high accuracy on NSL-KDD, CICIDS2017, Bot-IoT datasets
characteristics.context: Many frameworks evaluated on benchmark datasets; real-world deployment limited

characteristics.vs_baseline: Static anomaly detectors vs adaptive systems
characteristics.evidence: DFGL-LZTA integrates differential privacy, homomorphic encryption, DRL-based defence; most systems lack explicit poisoning mitigation
characteristics.context: Only DFGL-LZTA demonstrates comprehensive adversarial robustness; most IDS evaluated primarily on accuracy metrics

characteristics.what_it_measures: Edge deployment feasibility for constrained IoT devices
characteristics.evidence: COSIER uses ASCON cryptography and hierarchical ledgers; SecFedDNN uses layer-adaptive sparsified aggregation; Nicho et al. demonstrate CNN limitations on microcontrollers
characteristics.context: Lightweight blockchain and optimized ML score higher for efficiency; deep models often exceed resource constraints

characteristics.evidence: Belchior et al. address cross-chain trust exchange; Ferrag et al. demonstrate cross-dataset FL evaluation; most systems domain-specific
characteristics.context: Blockchain interoperability and standardized trust ontologies remain emerging; cross-domain benchmarking scarce

characteristics.what_it_measures: Balance between privacy protection and detection utility
characteristics.evidence: DFGL-LZTA combines differential privacy with homomorphic encryption; trade-offs between accuracy loss and protection levels documented
characteristics.context: Adaptive privacy mechanisms underexplored; formal privacy-utility analysis needed for IoT environments

novelty: Comprehensive taxonomy integrating FL, blockchain, ZTA, and graph-enhanced security
Multi-dimensional comparative analysis across trust accuracy, robustness, efficiency, and interoperability
Identifies convergence trend toward hybrid multi-layer decentralised architectures
Research roadmap addressing seven critical challenges for real-world deployment
open_problems: Robustness against poisoning, evasion, and backdoor attacks beyond accuracy-focused evaluation
Lightweight operation for resource-constrained IoT hardware including model compression techniques
Interoperability across administrative domains, ledgers, and trust models with standardized ontologies
Balancing privacy and utility in decentralised learning with adaptive mechanisms
Persistent multi-dimensional trust representation with temporal evolution and contextualization
Real-world deployment and long-term evaluation under operational constraints
Hybrid multi-layer architectures that integrate FL, blockchain, ZTA, and decentralized identity for edge environments
